broker_fee_detail: 'NO FEE' stated; also protected under FARE Act
broker_message: Hi Seth — interested in 2016 Albemarle #4D. Could you confirm it's rent-stabilized and share the current legal registered rent, whether the photos are of this exact unit, that heat/hot water are included in writing, and full move-in cost and lease term? Would the owner do $1,775? Thanks.
broker_questions: Is this rent-stabilized, and what's the current legal registered rent (DHCR)?
Are the photos of this actual unit?
What are the 6 litigation cases about?
How reliable is heat/hot water (advertised included)?
Full move-in cost and lease term?
Is it a separate-room studio or open layout?
building_risk_rationale: openigloo reports 10 open violations at 0.20/unit — below the NYC avg of 0.81 — which looks fine. But 6 of the 10 open are Class C (heat/hot water, pests, mold, defective parts), plus 2 evictions and 6 litigation cases, and a tenant review citing slow repairs. The Class C concentration and 6 litigation cases push to Medium despite the low per-unit count. Total-violations and 311/DOB counts behind paywall (unverified).
commute_notes: Church Ave and Beverley Rd on the B/Q Brighton Line are both a short walk. Q to Midtown ~35-45 min; strong Prospect Park access. Among the best commutes of the group.
concessions_to_ask: Fresh paint / minor repairs before move-in
cons: 6 open Class C violations and 6 litigation cases building-wide
Listing photos likely stale (2014/2016) — verify condition
Large 2016->2026 rent jump on a 'stabilized' unit needs legal-rent check
leverage_notes: Brand-new listing (0 days), rent-stabilized, heat+HW included, strong Q/B access — priced fairly at $1,825 vs comps $1,799-$2,050. Limited price leverage; focus on terms.
noise_risks: Albemarle Rd largely residential; nearby Church Ave can be busy
plain_english: A fresh, no-fee, rent-stabilized studio in a well-located Flatbush elevator building with heat and hot water included and great B/Q access near Prospect Park — attractive and fairly priced. Cautions: several immediately-hazardous (Class C) violations and multiple litigation cases, and the photos look years old. Tour the actual unit, get the stabilization/legal-rent history in writing, confirm heat reliability. If those check out, a strong option.
pros: Rent-stabilized + Good Cause building — renewal rights and capped increases
No fee, heat/hot water included, elevator + laundry + live-in super
Excellent B/Q access (Church Ave, Beverley Rd) near Prospect Park

red_flags.how_to_verify: Insist on seeing the actual unit in person
red_flags.issue: Listing photos may be reused from a 2016/2014 rental
red_flags.why: If photos are stale, actual condition may differ

red_flags.how_to_verify: Request DHCR rent history; confirm legal rent
red_flags.why: If stabilized, current rent should be supported by a legal registered rent; a large jump can signal preferential-rent or improper deregulation

red_flags.how_to_verify: Check HPD Online for live open C items; ask super about heat/pests/mold
red_flags.issue: 6 Class C open violations building-wide + 6 litigation cases
red_flags.why: Immediately-hazardous conditions and active litigation suggest friction

red_flags.issue: No sqft/floor plan despite 'LARGE STUDIO' claim

unverified: Exact total HPD count and 311/DOB/ECB counts (paywall)
Whether photos are current unit condition
Exact move-in costs and lease term
